paragraphs.text:
%md

# Netflix Content Analysis

**Course:** STQD6324 Data Management  
**Semester:** Semester 2, 2025/2026  
**Name:** Yang Huan  
**Matric Number:** P161796  
**Dataset:** Netflix Movies and TV Shows Dataset

msg.data:
Netflix Content Analysis

Course: STQD6324 Data Management
Semester: Semester 2, 2025/2026
Name: Yang Huan
Matric Number: P161796
Dataset: Netflix Movies and TV Shows Dataset

paragraphs.text:
%md

## 1.0 Introduction

Netflix is one of the major streaming platforms in the entertainment industry. It manages a large collection of movies and TV shows from different countries, genres and rating categories.

This project focuses on analyzing Netflix content data using Apache Zeppelin and Apache Hive. The purpose of this project is to manage, clean, query and analyze the Netflix dataset in order to understand content distribution, release trends and genre patterns.

msg.data:
1.0 Introduction

Netflix is one of the major streaming platforms in the entertainment industry. It manages a large collection of movies and TV shows from different countries, genres and rating categories.

This project focuses on analyzing Netflix content data using Apache Zeppelin and Apache Hive. The purpose of this project is to manage, clean, query and analyze the Netflix dataset in order to understand content distribution, release trends and genre patterns.

paragraphs.text:
%md

## 2.0 Dataset Source

The dataset used in this project is the Netflix Movies and TV Shows dataset from Kaggle.

- Dataset name: Netflix Movies and TV Shows
- File used: netflix_titles.csv
- Source: Kaggle
- Link: https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/shivamb/netflix-shows

The dataset contains information such as show ID, content type, title, director, cast, country, date added, release year, rating, duration, listed genre and description.

msg.data:
2.0 Dataset Source

The dataset used in this project is the Netflix Movies and TV Shows dataset from Kaggle.

  • Dataset name: Netflix Movies and TV Shows
  • File used: netflix_titles.csv
  • Source: Kaggle
  • Link: https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/shivamb/netflix-shows

The dataset contains information such as show ID, content type, title, director, cast, country, date added, release year, rating, duration, listed genre and description.

paragraphs.text:
%md

## 3.0 Tools and Data Management Process

This project uses Apache Zeppelin as the notebook environment and Apache Hive as the main data management tool. The raw Netflix CSV file is stored in HDFS and then accessed through an external Hive table.

The data management process includes uploading the dataset into HDFS, creating a Hive database, creating a Hive external table, and validating the data through Hive queries.

msg.data:
3.0 Tools and Data Management Process

This project uses Apache Zeppelin as the notebook environment and Apache Hive as the main data management tool. The raw Netflix CSV file is stored in HDFS and then accessed through an external Hive table.

The data management process includes uploading the dataset into HDFS, creating a Hive database, creating a Hive external table, and validating the data through Hive queries.

paragraphs.text:
%md

### 3.3 Hive Table Creation

An external Hive table was created to access the Netflix CSV file stored in HDFS. The OpenCSVSerde format was used because the dataset contains text fields with commas and quotation marks.

msg.data:
3.3 Hive Table Creation

An external Hive table was created to access the Netflix CSV file stored in HDFS. The OpenCSVSerde format was used because the dataset contains text fields with commas and quotation marks.

paragraphs.text:
%sh
hive -e "
USE netflix_final;

DROP TABLE IF EXISTS netflix_titles;

CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE netflix_titles (
  show_id STRING,
  content_type STRING,
  title STRING,
  director STRING,
  cast_list STRING,
  country STRING,
  date_added STRING,
  release_year INT,
  rating STRING,
  duration STRING,
  listed_in STRING,
  description STRING
)
ROW FORMAT SERDE 'org.apache.hadoop.hive.serde2.OpenCSVSerde'
WITH SERDEPROPERTIES (
  'separatorChar' = ',',
  'quoteChar' = '\"'
)
STORED AS TEXTFILE
LOCATION '/user/maria_dev/final_report/netflix'
TBLPROPERTIES ('skip.header.line.count'='1');

SHOW TABLES;
"

## 4.0 Data Cleaning

Before performing analysis, the dataset was checked for missing values and invalid records. Data cleaning is important because incomplete values in fields such as director, cast, country, rating and duration may affect the quality of analysis.

msg.data:
4.0 Data Cleaning

Before performing analysis, the dataset was checked for missing values and invalid records. Data cleaning is important because incomplete values in fields such as director, cast, country, rating and duration may affect the quality of analysis.

paragraphs.text:
%sh
hive -e "
USE netflix_final;

SELECT 
  COUNT(*) AS total_records,
  SUM(CASE WHEN director IS NULL OR TRIM(director) = '' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS missing_director,
  SUM(CASE WHEN cast_list IS NULL OR TRIM(cast_list) = '' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS missing_cast,
  SUM(CASE WHEN country IS NULL OR TRIM(country) = '' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS missing_country,
  SUM(CASE WHEN date_added IS NULL OR TRIM(date_added) = '' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS missing_date_added,
  SUM(CASE WHEN rating IS NULL OR TRIM(rating) = '' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS missing_rating,
  SUM(CASE WHEN duration IS NULL OR TRIM(duration) = '' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS missing_duration
FROM netflix_titles;
"

paragraphs.text:
%md

### 4.1 Create Cleaned Hive Table

After checking missing values, a cleaned Hive table is created. Missing values in director, cast and country are replaced with `Unknown` because these fields are useful for analysis but not always available. Records with missing date_added, rating or duration are removed because these fields are important for time, rating and duration analysis.

msg.data:
4.1 Create Cleaned Hive Table

After checking missing values, a cleaned Hive table is created. Missing values in director, cast and country are replaced with Unknown because these fields are useful for analysis but not always available. Records with missing date_added, rating or duration are removed because these fields are important for time, rating and duration analysis.

paragraphs.text:
%sh
hive -e "
USE netflix_final;

DROP TABLE IF EXISTS netflix_titles_cleaned;

CREATE TABLE netflix_titles_cleaned AS
SELECT DISTINCT
  show_id,
  content_type,
  title,
  CASE 
    WHEN director IS NULL OR TRIM(director) = '' THEN 'Unknown'
    ELSE director
  END AS director,
  CASE 
    WHEN cast_list IS NULL OR TRIM(cast_list) = '' THEN 'Unknown'
    ELSE cast_list
  END AS cast_list,
  CASE 
    WHEN country IS NULL OR TRIM(country) = '' THEN 'Unknown'
    ELSE country
  END AS country,
  date_added,
  release_year,
  rating,
  duration,
  listed_in,
  description
FROM netflix_titles
WHERE show_id RLIKE '^s[0-9]+$'
  AND content_type IN ('Movie', 'TV Show')
  AND title IS NOT NULL AND TRIM(title) <> ''
  AND date_added IS NOT NULL AND TRIM(date_added) <> ''
  AND rating IS NOT NULL AND TRIM(rating) <> ''
  AND duration IS NOT NULL AND TRIM(duration) <> '';

SELECT COUNT(*) AS cleaned_records
FROM netflix_titles_cleaned;
"

## 5.0 Data Visualization and Analysis

After data cleaning, several analytical queries are performed to understand the content patterns in the Netflix dataset. The analysis focuses on content type, country distribution, rating categories, release year trends and genre patterns.

msg.data:
5.0 Data Visualization and Analysis

After data cleaning, several analytical queries are performed to understand the content patterns in the Netflix dataset. The analysis focuses on content type, country distribution, rating categories, release year trends and genre patterns.

paragraphs.text:
%sh
echo -e "%table\ncontent_type\ttotal_count"
hive -S -e "
USE netflix_final;

SELECT content_type, COUNT(*) AS total_count
FROM netflix_titles_cleaned
GROUP BY content_type
ORDER BY total_count DESC;
" 2>/dev/null

msg.data:
content_type	total_count
Movie	6125
TV Show	2664

paragraphs.text:
%md

### Interpretation

The result shows that movies make up the majority of Netflix content, with 6,125 movie titles compared with 2,664 TV shows. This indicates that Netflix has a larger movie catalogue in this dataset. However, TV shows still represent an important part of the platform because they can attract long-term viewing through multiple seasons and episodes.

msg.data:
Interpretation

The result shows that movies make up the majority of Netflix content, with 6,125 movie titles compared with 2,664 TV shows. This indicates that Netflix has a larger movie catalogue in this dataset. However, TV shows still represent an important part of the platform because they can attract long-term viewing through multiple seasons and episodes.

paragraphs.text:
%sh
echo -e "%table\ncountry\ttotal_count"
hive -S -e "
USE netflix_final;

SELECT country, COUNT(*) AS total_count
FROM netflix_titles_cleaned
WHERE country <> 'Unknown'
GROUP BY country
ORDER BY total_count DESC
LIMIT 10;
" 2>/dev/null

This suggests that Netflix content is strongly concentrated in major media-producing countries. India also has a large content contribution, showing the importance of regional and international content in Netflix's catalogue.

%sh

hive -S -e "
USE netflix_final;

SELECT release_year, COUNT(*) AS total_count
FROM netflix_titles_cleaned
WHERE release_year >= 2000
GROUP BY release_year
ORDER BY release_year;
" 2>/dev/null

%md

### Interpretation

The release year trend shows that most Netflix titles in the dataset were released after 2000, especially in the 2010s and early 2020s. This indicates that Netflix's catalogue mainly focuses on relatively recent content. Newer titles are more likely to match current audience interests and streaming market demand.

This indicates that Netflix’s catalogue mainly focuses on relatively recent content. Newer titles are more likely to match current audience interests and streaming market demand.

paragraphs.text:
%sh
echo -e "%table\ngenre_name\ttotal_count"
hive -S -e "
USE netflix_final;

SELECT 
  TRIM(genre) AS genre_name,
  COUNT(*) AS total_count
FROM netflix_titles_cleaned
LATERAL VIEW explode(split(listed_in, ',')) genre_table AS genre
GROUP BY TRIM(genre)
ORDER BY total_count DESC
LIMIT 10;
" 2>/dev/null

The genre analysis shows the most common content categories in the Netflix dataset. Genres such as International Movies, Dramas and Comedies usually appear frequently, which suggests that Netflix provides a wide range of global and entertainment-focused content. Interpretation

The genre analysis shows the most common content categories in the Netflix dataset. Genres such as International Movies, Dramas and Comedies usually appear frequently, which suggests that Netflix provides a wide range of global and entertainment-focused content. This also shows that genre diversity is important for attracting audiences from different countries and age groups.

paragraphs.text:
%sh
echo -e "%table\nduration_group\ttotal_count"
hive -S -e "
USE netflix_final;

SELECT 
  CASE
    WHEN CAST(regexp_extract(duration, '[0-9]+', 0) AS INT) < 60 THEN 'Less than 60 min'
    WHEN CAST(regexp_extract(duration, '[0-9]+', 0) AS INT) BETWEEN 60 AND 90 THEN '60-90 min'
    WHEN CAST(regexp_extract(duration, '[0-9]+', 0) AS INT) BETWEEN 91 AND 120 THEN '91-120 min'
    ELSE 'More than 120 min'
  END AS duration_group,
  COUNT(*) AS total_count
FROM netflix_titles_cleaned
WHERE content_type = 'Movie'
  AND duration LIKE '%min'
GROUP BY 
  CASE
    WHEN CAST(regexp_extract(duration, '[0-9]+', 0) AS INT) < 60 THEN 'Less than 60 min'
    WHEN CAST(regexp_extract(duration, '[0-9]+', 0) AS INT) BETWEEN 60 AND 90 THEN '60-90 min'
    WHEN CAST(regexp_extract(duration, '[0-9]+', 0) AS INT) BETWEEN 91 AND 120 THEN '91-120 min'
    ELSE 'More than 120 min'
  END
ORDER BY total_count DESC;
" 2>/dev/null

msg.data:
duration_group	total_count
91-120 min	2995
60-90 min	1532
More than 120 min	1142
Less than 60 min	456

paragraphs.text:
%md

### Interpretation

The movie duration analysis shows how Netflix movies are distributed by running time. This analysis is useful because movie length can affect viewing behaviour and content planning. If most movies fall within the 91-120 minute group, it suggests that Netflix's movie catalogue mainly follows the common feature film duration range.%sh

paragraphs.text:
%md

## 6.0 Key Findings and Discussion

Based on the analysis, several important findings can be observed from the Netflix dataset.

First, movies make up a larger proportion of the dataset than TV shows. There are 6,125 movie titles and 2,664 TV shows in the cleaned dataset. This indicates that Netflix has a stronger movie catalogue, although TV shows remain important for long-term audience engagement.

Second, the United States contributes the highest number of titles, followed by India and the United Kingdom. This shows that Netflix content is strongly concentrated in major content-producing countries, while still maintaining an international content strategy.

Third, the most common ratings are TV-MA and TV-14. This suggests that much of the Netflix catalogue is targeted at mature audiences and teenagers, while children and family-friendly ratings appear less frequently.

Fourth, most titles were released after 2000, especially between 2015 and 2020. This indicates that Netflix mainly focuses on newer and more recent content to match current audience demand.

Fifth, the genre analysis shows that International Movies, Dramas and Comedies are the dominant categories. This suggests that Netflix places strong emphasis on global entertainment content and broad audience appeal.

In addition, the use of HDFS, Hive and Zeppelin helped organize the raw CSV dataset into a structured format. This made the data easier to clean, query, analyze and visualize in a repeatable way.

msg.data:
6.0 Key Findings and Discussion

Based on the analysis, several important findings can be observed from the Netflix dataset.

First, movies make up a larger proportion of the dataset than TV shows. There are 6,125 movie titles and 2,664 TV shows in the cleaned dataset. This indicates that Netflix has a stronger movie catalogue, although TV shows remain important for long-term audience engagement.

Second, the United States contributes the highest number of titles, followed by India and the United Kingdom. This shows that Netflix content is strongly concentrated in major content-producing countries, while still maintaining an international content strategy.

Third, the most common ratings are TV-MA and TV-14. This suggests that much of the Netflix catalogue is targeted at mature audiences and teenagers, while children and family-friendly ratings appear less frequently.

Fourth, most titles were released after 2000, especially between 2015 and 2020. This indicates that Netflix mainly focuses on newer and more recent content to match current audience demand.

Fifth, the genre analysis shows that International Movies, Dramas and Comedies are the dominant categories. This suggests that Netflix places strong emphasis on global entertainment content and broad audience appeal.

In addition, the use of HDFS, Hive and Zeppelin helped organize the raw CSV dataset into a structured format. This made the data easier to clean, query, analyze and visualize in a repeatable way.

paragraphs.text:
%md

## 7.0 Recommendations

Based on the findings, several recommendations can be suggested for Netflix content management.

1. Netflix should continue expanding international content because global genres such as International Movies and International TV Shows are highly represented and appear to be important in the catalogue.

2. Netflix may strengthen its TV show library further, since TV shows can improve audience retention and long-term engagement.

3. Since mature ratings such as TV-MA and TV-14 dominate the dataset, Netflix could consider maintaining a better balance by increasing family-friendly and children’s content.

4. Netflix should continue investing in recently released content because newer titles are more likely to attract current audience interest.

5. Netflix can also use genre-based strategies to improve recommendations, especially for popular categories such as drama, comedy and documentaries.

msg.data:
7.0 Recommendations

Based on the findings, several recommendations can be suggested for Netflix content management.

  1. Netflix should continue expanding international content because global genres such as International Movies and International TV Shows are highly represented and appear to be important in the catalogue.

  2. Netflix may strengthen its TV show library further, since TV shows can improve audience retention and long-term engagement.

  3. Since mature ratings such as TV-MA and TV-14 dominate the dataset, Netflix could consider maintaining a better balance by increasing family-friendly and children’s content.

  4. Netflix should continue investing in recently released content because newer titles are more likely to attract current audience interest.

  5. Netflix can also use genre-based strategies to improve recommendations, especially for popular categories such as drama, comedy and documentaries.

paragraphs.text:
%md

## 8.0 Conclusion

In conclusion, this project analyzed the Netflix Movies and TV Shows dataset using Apache Zeppelin and Apache Hive. The project covered the full workflow from storing the dataset in HDFS, creating Hive tables, performing data cleaning, and conducting analytical queries.

The findings show that Netflix content is dominated by movies, strongly influenced by the United States and India, and mainly targeted at teenage and mature audiences. The dataset also shows that Netflix focuses heavily on recent content and popular genres such as international movies, dramas and comedies.

Overall, this project demonstrates how data management tools such as Hive and Zeppelin can be used to manage and analyze real-world industry datasets effectively.

msg.data:
8.0 Conclusion

In conclusion, this project analyzed the Netflix Movies and TV Shows dataset using Apache Zeppelin and Apache Hive. The project covered the full workflow from storing the dataset in HDFS, creating Hive tables, performing data cleaning, and conducting analytical queries.

The findings show that Netflix content is dominated by movies, strongly influenced by the United States and India, and mainly targeted at teenage and mature audiences. The dataset also shows that Netflix focuses heavily on recent content and popular genres such as international movies, dramas and comedies.

Overall, this project demonstrates how data management tools such as Hive and Zeppelin can be used to manage and analyze real-world industry datasets effectively.

paragraphs.text: %md
